data: Marble plaque set in plaster in modern times.

Regular letters: rhomboid Θ, Ο, Ω (open); Α with accentuated upper serif, Μ with slanting full-length bars (but irregular), Υ with an extended right arm (line 6), ΗΝΕ at l. 4 and ΤΗ at l. 5 (x2) in ligature. In the last line, some letters are smaller than others for lack of space.
Second half of 1st — end 2nd century CE
Original discovery not recorded, probably from Catania..
Observed by Kaibel in Museo dei Benedettini, later by Korhonen in magazzino del cortile, Collezione dei Benedettini (formerly sala VI.50)
I.Sicily with the permission of the Assessorato Regionale dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana - Dipartimento dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana
Castello Ursino, Piazza Federico II di Svevia, 95121 Catania (CT)
Inscriptiones Graecae Siciliae et Italiae, additis graecis Galliae Hispaniae, Britanniae, Germaniae inscriptionibus

G. Kaibel, Inscriptiones Graecae Siciliae et Italiae, additis graecis Galliae Hispaniae, Britanniae, Germaniae inscriptionibus, Inscriptiones Graecae consilio et auctoritate Academiae Litterarum Regiae Borussicae Editae. Volumen XIV., XIV (Berlin: Georgius Reimerus, 1890)
Illustrazione d'alcune epigrafi e d'altri oggetti archeologici
G. De Spuches, «Illustrazione d’alcune epigrafi e d’altri oggetti archeologici», Bullettino della Commissione di Antichità e belle arti in Sicilia 1 (1864): 12–17
Le iscrizioni del Museo civico di Catania : storia delle collezioni, cultura epigrafica, edizione
Kalle Korhonen, Le iscrizioni del Museo civico di Catania : storia delle collezioni, cultura epigrafica, edizione (Helsinki: Societas Scientiarum Fennica, 2004)

Text encoding: Victoria Fendel, Robert Crellin
ΘΕΟΙΣΚΑΤΑΧΘΟΝΙΟΙΣΚΑΣΣΙΑΒΕΝΟΥΣΤΑΕΖΗΣΕΝΕΤΗΕΝΔΕΚΑΜΗΝΕΣΘΗΜΕΡΕΣΗΠΑΤΗΡΚΑΙΜΗΤΗΡΤΕΚΝΩΓΛΥΚΥΤΑΤΩΕΠΟΙΗΣΑΝ

Facsimile Images

Apparatus criticus

  • Text based on photographs ;
  • line.5: Kaibel saw an ivy leaf at the end of the line, but it rather seems to be a triangle-shaped interpunct.

English translation

Marta Fogagnolo

To the underworld spirits. Kassia Benousta, lived for 11 years, 9 months, and 8 days. Her father and mother made this for their sweetest child.

Italian translation

Marta Fogagnolo

Agli Dei inferi. Kassia Benousta visse 11 anni, 9 mesi e 8 giorni. Suo padre e sua madre realizzarono questo per la loro dolce figlia.

commentary

The structure of the epitaph is the most frequent in pagan epitaphs, with the invocation to the underworld spirits, the name of the deceased in the nominative form, his/her age, the name of the dedicants (in this case, the parents) and the dedication verb in aorist (Korhonen 2004: 85-86). An interesting linguistic detail is the indication of months and days in the nominative and not in the accusative form (μῆνες, ἡμέρες), which seems to be due to confusion with Latin where the nominative and accusative plural of mensis and dies correspond: for a similar confusion, see also SEG 44.0762 = ISic000641. On the name Βενοῦστα, see SEG 29.931-3 (from Morgantina, 1st century CE). Note too the spelling out of the first numeral in full, rather than using the standard alphabetic notation.

bibliography

  • Spuches 1864 13 no.3 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10002042
  • Kaibel 1890 483 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10001072
  • Korhonen 2004 67 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10000028
  • Korhonen 2004 85-86 https://biblio.inscriptiones.org/epig10000028

Text based on photographs Kaibel saw an ivy leaf at the end of the line, but it rather seems to be a triangle-shaped interpunct.

To the underworld spirits. Kassia Benousta, lived for 11 years, 9 months, and 8 days. Her father and mother made this for their sweetest child.

Agli Dei inferi. Kassia Benousta visse 11 anni, 9 mesi e 8 giorni. Suo padre e sua madre realizzarono questo per la loro dolce figlia.

The structure of the epitaph is the most frequent in pagan epitaphs, with the invocation to the underworld spirits, the name of the deceased in the nominative form, his/her age, the name of the dedicants (in this case, the parents) and the dedication verb in aorist (Korhonen 2004: 85-86). An interesting linguistic detail is the indication of months and days in the nominative and not in the accusative form (μῆνες, ἡμέρες), which seems to be due to confusion with Latin where the nominative and accusative plural of mensis and dies correspond: for a similar confusion, see also SEG 44.0762 = ISic000641. On the name Βενοῦστα, see SEG 29.931-3 (from Morgantina, 1st century CE). Note too the spelling out of the first numeral in full, rather than using the standard alphabetic notation.
